pi_items.source: Verified by checking task.json description and setup_task.sh where OUTPUT_FILE is explicitly defined.
Verified by downloading the 0051.zip dataset from the InVesalius GitHub releases and confirming it extracts to a folder named 0051 within the ct_cranium path.
Verified by running a Python script with pydicom to load all 108 downloaded DICOM files and explicitly checking their Dimensions (512x512).
Verified by reading the task description and verifier.py logic, which strictly tests if the bottom 10 slices are fully erased.
Verified by analyzing the center slice (index 54) using pydicom, calculating that a 226-3071 HU threshold yields 17199 bone voxels, safely exceeding the minimum threshold required by the verifier.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a real InVesalius CT Cranium sample dataset (0051.zip). Analysis of the raw DICOM files confirms they have dimensions of 512x512 and consist of 108 slices. The center slice contains over 17,000 bone voxels when standard bone Hounsfield Units (226-3071) are applied, exceeding the task's 1000-voxel minimum threshold. The task strictly requires clearing the bone mask from the bottom 10 slices and saving the NIfTI to /home/ga/Documents/cleaned_skull.nii.gz.
